_note: THE KINGS OF BABYLON — a SEPARATE Mesopotamian succession island (ids prefixed BAB_), deliberately NOT linked by bloodline to Adam's line or to the other king-list islands. Dynasties here were repeatedly broken by conquest, so this is a SUCCESSION chain (a king-list), not one continuous bloodline; only the runs noted below are true father-son descent. It spans from the Old Babylonian First (Amorite) Dynasty of Hammurabi, through long foreign/broken interludes rendered as single bridge nodes (Kassite Dynasty, Second Dynasty of Isin, and the chaotic 8th-7th c.), down to the Neo-Babylonian (Chaldean) empire and its fall to Cyrus the Great in 539 BC. Chain is ordered by strictly descending date (earliest BC at top). REAL father-son descent: Sumu-abum is Sumu-la-El's predecessor but Sin-muballit was Hammurabi's actual father; Hammurabi→Samsu-iluna, Nabopolassar→Nebuchadnezzar II→Amel-Marduk, and Nabonidus→Belshazzar are genuine father-son links; the other steps are dynastic succession, including usurpations. Old Babylonian dates follow the Middle Chronology and are scholarly approximations. Ties tie to Nabonidus: he acceded in 556 BC, the same year as Labashi-Marduk's fall, and is shown here as c.555 only to keep the chain strictly decreasing (his standard reign is 556-539 BC). Sources: Wikipedia 'Sumu-abum', 'Sin-muballit', 'Hammurabi', 'Samsu-iluna', 'Old Babylonian Empire', 'Mursili I', 'Kassite dynasty', 'Second Dynasty of Isin', 'Nebuchadnezzar I', 'Neo-Babylonian Empire', 'Chaldean dynasty', 'Nabopolassar', 'Nebuchadnezzar II', 'Amel-Marduk', 'Neriglissar', 'Labashi-Marduk', 'Nabonidus', 'Belshazzar'; World History Encyclopedia 'Hammurabi', 'Babylon'.

children.label:
👑 Hammurabi
c.1792 BC · the great lawgiver; unified Mesopotamia and issued the Code of Hammurabi · historical

👑 Samsu-iluna
c.1749 BC · son of Hammurabi; reign marked by revolts and the loss of the south · historical

Hittite Sack of Babylon
c.1595 BC · Mursili I of the Hittites sacked Babylon, ending the First Dynasty & the Old Babylonian period · historical event

Kassite Dynasty
c.1570 BC · long foreign rule of Babylon by a Kassite dynasty (consolidated after the Hittites withdrew, to c.1155 BC); its longest & most stable era · historical (bridge)

Second Dynasty of Isin
c.1155-1025 BC · native revival; its greatest king Nebuchadnezzar I (c.1125 BC) crushed Elam & recovered Marduk's statue · historical (bridge)

Chaotic period
c.1025-627 BC · centuries of weakness, Aramean & Chaldean pressure, and Assyrian domination of Babylon · historical (bridge)

👑 Nabopolassar
c.626 BC · founder of the Neo-Babylonian (Chaldean) empire; threw off Assyria & destroyed Nineveh with the Medes (612 BC) · historical

👑 Nebuchadnezzar II
c.605 BC · son of Nabopolassar; destroyed Jerusalem & the Temple (587 BC) and carried Judah into the Babylonian Exile · historical

👑 Amel-Marduk
c.562 BC · son of Nebuchadnezzar II; the 'Evil-Merodach' who freed King Jehoiachin of Judah from prison · historical

👑 Neriglissar
c.560 BC · general who murdered & usurped Amel-Marduk; dynastic break · historical

👑 Labashi-Marduk
c.556 BC · boy-king son of Neriglissar; reigned only a few months before being overthrown · historical

👑 Nabonidus
c.555 BC · last king of Babylon (reign 556-539 BC); usurper who withdrew to Tayma leaving his son as regent · historical

👑 Belshazzar
c.553 BC · son & co-regent of Nabonidus; the king of Daniel 5's 'writing on the wall'; Babylon fell to Cyrus the Great in 539 BC · historical
